title: Handwriting — By Witness
text: A writing may be authenticated by a witness who states either of the following: (a) The handwriting is that of the person whose handwriting it purports to be. (b) The witness, during the course of a relationship or following an occurrence or transaction, has acquired knowledge of a person's handwriting, and the handwriting is that of the person.

structure.article_title: Means of Authenticating and Proving Writings
